Many-objective evolutionary algorithm based on three-way decision
Zhihua Cui, Bingting Li ⇑, Zhuoxuan Lan, Yubin Xu
Shanxi Key Laboratory of Big Data Analysis and Parallel Computing, Taiyuan University of Science and Technology, China




a r t i c l e        i n f o                           a b s t r a c t

Article history:                                       In recent years, many-objective optimization problems have been widely used. however, with the
Received 20 February 2023                              increase of the number of objectives, the difficulty of solving increases exponentially, and the imbalance
Revised 23 May 2023                                    between convergence and diversity becomes more serious. In view of the above problems, this paper
Accepted 9 June 2023
                                                       combines the idea of three-way decision, redesigns the environment selection strategy, and proposes a
Available online 30 June 2023
                                                       many-objective optimization algorithm based on three-way decision. Firstly, the distance from the indi-
                                                       vidual to the ideal point is used as an index to measure individual convergence, the minimum distance
Keywords:
                                                       from the individual to other solutions is used as an indicator to measure individual diversity, and the
Many-objective optimization problems
Many-objective evolutionary algorithm
                                                       individuals with good convergence and good diversity are selected separately by combining the thresh-
Three-way decision                                     olds of the three-way decision; and secondly, A dynamic threshold acquisition method is designed to fur-
Convergence                                            ther improve the performance of the algorithm; Finally, it is proved that the algorithm can effectively
Diversity                                              balance convergence and diversity through tests of different data sets, so as to verify the feasibility
                                                       and effectiveness of the algorithm
                                                       Ó 2023 Production and hosting by Elsevier B.V. on behalf of Faculty of Computers and Information, Cairo
                                                          University. This is an open access article under the CC BY-NC-ND license (http://creativecommons.org/
                                                                                                                                          licenses/by-nc-nd/4.0/).




1. Introduction                                                                           strategies to select good-quality individuals. GrEA combines Pareto
                                                                                          domination with grid sorting to select individuals with good con-
    In real life, Multi-objective Optimization Problems (MOPs) are                        vergence and uses grid crowding distance to select individuals
prevalent. In MOPs [1–4,36,37], the objective functions are in con-                       with good diversity. NSGA-III algorithm uses a non-dominant sort-
flict with each other, that is, each objective function cannot reach                      ing strategy and reference point strategy. However, with the
the optimal solution at the same time, but there is a set of compro-                      increase in the number of iterations, the number of non-
mise solutions called the pareto optimal solution. At present, intel-                     dominant solutions in the population will gradually increase. How-
ligent optimization algorithms are mainly used to solve MOPs,                             ever, the reference point strategy only considers the diversity of
such as Genetic Algorithm (GA) [5], Ant Clony Optimization                                the population. Therefore, common environmental selection meth-
(ACO) [6], Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) [7] and so on. When                          ods will lead to population convergence and diversity will be out of
solving MOPs (i.e. 2  3 objectives), this kind of algorithm has the                      balance. Aiming at how to better balance individual convergence
advantages of high efficiency and strong adaptability, and can be                         and diversity choice in environment selection, the three-way deci-
handled efficiently without interference from various solving                             sion provides a possible idea.
problems.                                                                                     The Three-Way Decision (TWD) theory [11] is a decision-
    However, most of the optimization problems in real life belong                        making model developed on the basis of rough set theory [12] pro-
to (the number of objectives is greater than 3) Many-objective                            posed by Yiyu Yao, which is more in line with human cognition and
Optimization Problems (MaOPs) [10], at this time, the objective                           efficient. It believes that there is enough information to judge
spatial dimension increases exponentially, the difficulty of solving                      whether things are good or bad, to make decisions about accep-
increases, the convergence and diversity of balanced solutions are                        tance or rejection. For some problems that do not have enough
more difficult [17], and the algorithm for solving MOPS is no longer                      information to make decisions, you can put things into the bound-
applicable. Among the common MaOEA algorithms, MOEA/D uses                                ary domain and delay the judgment of things, which is called
Pareto domination and penalty-based boundary intersection                                 delayed decision-making. When the evidence is sufficient and
                                                                                          complete, make an acceptance or rejection strategy. In environ-
                                                                                          mental selection, the distance from an individual to an ideal point

2. Related work                                                            set was adjusted according to the index contribution of the candi-
                                                                           date solution to enhance the universality of understanding [27].

algorithm based on the selection mechanism. There are mainly               generation. Using NSGA-III of the reference point strategy, EFRRR

       based on Pareto domination.                                         between diversity and convergence among solutions. Cheng [33]

selection pressure by modifying the Pareto domination relation             vectors.
[31]. Sato [25] expanded or contracted the dominant area of indi-
viduals by modifying the objective value with customized param-              (4) High-dimensional many-objective evolutionary algorithm
eters, thus changing the dominant relationship between                           based on fusion selection strategy.
individuals. Sun [26] selected inverted generational distance solu-
                                                                               This algorithm combines different selection mechanisms and
tions with good convergence and diversity based on the IGD index,
                                                                           uses their respective advantages to balance diversity and conver-
proposed a new dominant relationship, and developed an adaptive
                                                                           gence. The improved double archiving algorithm Two_Arch2 [34]
niche technology based on the perspectives of the candidate solu-
                                                                           is a low complexity algorithm, it has two archives, and allocates
tions, to improve the accuracy of IGD sampling reference points.
                                                                           different selection principles to them, namely two indicators and
Among algorithms based on Pareto domination, most MOEA shows
                                                                       2
Z. Cui, B. Li, Z. Lan et al.                                                                                Egyptian Informatics Journal 24 (2023) 100388


Pareto based environment selection strategies, focusing on conver-
gence and diversity respectively. SPEA is proposed to find or
approximate Pareto optimal set of multi-objective optimization
problems. Zitzler proposed SPEA2 [24], which added two new
mechanisms to improve its searching ability, one is a more effec-
tive crossover mechanism, and the other is an archiving mecha-
nism to maintain the diversity of solutions in the objective space
and variable space. Selection strategies, including mating selection
and environmental selection, are key factors in the design of many-
objective evolutionary algorithm. In MaOEA-CSS [35], the algo-
rithm takes into account the quality of each selected parent and
the effectiveness of the selected parent combination as well as
the performance of the population as a whole. At the same time,
new measures of distance convergence and angle differentiation
are proposed to solve the dimension problem in multi-objective
optimization problems. The convergence index of the computa-
tional efficiency of the algorithm selects descendant individuals
one by one to increase the selection pressure toward the Pareto
optimal frontier.


3. Many-objective evolutionary algorithm based on three-way
decision

   This chapter details the Many-objective evolutionary algorithm
based on three-way decision (MaOEA-TWD). The idea of three-way
decision is adopted to balance convergence and diversity in the
solution process. Then, a dynamic threshold acquisition method
of three-way decisions is proposed to enhance the performance
of the algorithm. The basic steps of the algorithm are described
in 3.1. Dynamic threshold acquisition methods are described in
detail in 3.2. Time complexity analysis are described in detail in
3.3.


3.1. MaOEA-TWD algorithm

    With the increase of the number of objectives, the difficulty of
solving increases, and the conflict between convergence and diver-
                                                                                        Fig. 2. MaOEA-TWD algorithm model framework.
sity becomes more intense, this paper combines the ideas of three-
day decisions, redesigns the environment selection strategy, and
proposes a many-objective optimization algorithm based on                  three-way decision (MaOEA-TWD), the basic steps of which are
                                                                           shown in Fig. 2.
                                                                              Step 1: Initialize population N, generate first-generation sub-
                                                                           groups, generate reference points and ideal points;
                                                                              Step 2: Parent and offspring combine Rt ¼ P t [ Q t (size N) and
                                                                           select new parents (N) from among them;
                                                                              Step 3: Divide 2 N individuals into multiple nondominated lay-
                                                                           ers (Z1, Z2, . . .) by nondominated ordering, and each layer will have
                                                                           H individuals. Individuals are then picked from the first layer to
                                                                           construct a new population until the population exceeds N or
                                                                           equals N for the first time, as shown in Fig. 1.
                                                                              Step 4: Make your selection. At the L layer, the population size
                                                                           exceeds N or equals N for the first time. Then the L layer is called
                                                                           the critical layer, and the solution above the critical layer will be




                         Fig. 1. Eliminate the critical layer above.                      Fig. 3. Three domains for three-way decision.

                                                                       3
Z. Cui, B. Li, Z. Lan et al.                                                                                                       Egyptian Informatics Journal 24 (2023) 100388


eliminated, where k = (N-L*H) individuals in the critical layer will                 The three domains satisfy the following relationships:
be accepted.
                                                                                  U ¼P[N[B                                                                                  ð3Þ
    Step 5: Set the convergence and diversity thresholds, and calculate
the convergence and diversity of critical layer individuals, using the
                                                                                  P\N ¼£                                                                                    ð4Þ
distance from the individual to the ideal point to represent the conver-
gence of the individual, and the minimum distance from the individ-
                                                                                  N\B¼£                                                                                     ð5Þ
ual to other solutions to express the diversity of the individual.
    Step 6: Compare the convergence and diversity of critical layer               P\B¼£                                                                                     ð6Þ
individuals with convergence threshold and diversity threshold,
put individuals with good convergence and diversity into P domain                     Let the current number of iterations be t, there are N x individu-
and N domain, and put the rest into B domain, if k is less than the               als in the critical layer, and the convergence and diversity of the
sum of P domain plus N domain, then select k individuals in P domain              critical layer individuals in the current number of iterations are
and N domain, and form a new population with the former L-1 layer,                calculated respectively, the maximum and minimum values of

   The individuals in domains P, B and N are a, b and c. If the value                                             

of k is less than a + c, k individuals are randomly selected in the P                   max ðdt  dt Þðdt  dt Þ

and N domains to form new progeny. If the value of k is greater                                           min
                                                                                                  ðdt  dt ÞNx
than a + c, domain B is treated as the critical layer and three deci-
sions continue to be made until k individuals are selected.                           According to the above formula, the diversity and convergence
                                                                                  of individuals in the critical layer are compared successively, and
3.2.2. The setting of dynamic thresholds                                          the P domain, B domain and N domain are divided. If k individuals
   The setting of threshold has always been the focus of three-way                cannot be selected in the P and N domains, the thresholds at and bt
decision research, and the traditional threshold setting method                   are indented to E and F respectively, and the B domains are divided
needs to prioritize the loss function of the given dataset, which                 into new P, B, and N domains, and individuals are continued to be
cannot meet the application of the three-way decision on many-                    selected until k individuals are selected, and then subsequent steps
objective optimization models, and in the many-objective opti-                    of the algorithm are carried out.
mization algorithm, the convergence and diversity thresholds of
the population change with the change of the number of popula-                    3.3. Time complexity analysis
tion iterations. Therefore, a three-way decision dynamic threshold
acquisition method is designed.                                                      In this section, the time complexity of the proposed MaOEA-
   Let U=(x,x,. . ... . ..., x) is a non-empty finite set of objects. These       TWD algorithm is analyzed. The main steps of the algorithm
three decisions divide the entity set U into three disjoint domains               include population initialization, population selection, crossover
P, N and B by the convergence and diversity thresholds atand bt,                  and mutation operations, fast non-dominated sorting, population
respectively.                                                                     convergence and diversity calculation, and linear threshold three-
  at ;bt                                                                          way decision selection strategy. Assuming that population of size
U ! fP; N; Bg                                                          ð2Þ
                                                                                  is N, the objective dimension is M. So, this algorithm to select

the operation time complexity is O (Nlog2 N), crossover operation                          Therefore, these benchmark questions are challenging to evaluate
time complexity is O (1), the variation of time complexity is O (1),                       the performance of MaOEAs. The specific characteristics of DTLZ
fast non dominated sorting operation time complexity is O (MN2),                           and WFG test sets are shown in Table 1.
the time complexity of calculating population convergence and
diversity is O(2 N), and the operation time complexity of three-                             (1) Parameter setting
way decision selection strategies with linear threshold is O(N2).

                                                                                           compared on the test data set DTLZi (i = 1, 2,. . .,7) and WFGi (i = 1,

   DTLZ4              4,6,8,10,15              120,132,156,275,135    10,000               2,. . .,6). Heuristic threshold means that the histogram is divided
   DTLZ5              4,6,8,10,15              120,132,156,275,135    10,000               into two groups at a certain threshold. When the variance between
   DTLZ6              4,6,8,10,15              120,132,156,275,135    10,000               the two groups is the largest, the threshold is determined. The
   DTLZ7              4,6,8,10,15              120,132,156,275,135    10,000               average threshold means that the average convergence or diversity
   WFG1               4,6,8,10,15              120,132,156,275,135    10,000
                                                                                           of a population is taken as the threshold. Set M = 4, then 13 differ-
   WFG2               4,6,8,10,15              120,132,156,275,135    10,000

   WFG2                                      8.5546e + 3(1.07e-1) -                           6.7959e + 3 (6.98e-2) -                         5.3283e-1 (1.13e-1)

the six algorithms. +‘‘ indicates that this algorithm is superior to                              tion strategy based on a three-way decision plays an obvious role
MaOEA-TWD, ”-‘‘ indicates that it is inferior to MaOEA-TWD, and                                   in alleviating the conflict between algorithm diversity and conver-
”=‘‘ indicates that this algorithm is equal to MaOEA-TWD. By com-                                 gence. Compared with the other five algorithms, MaOEA-TWD
paring six different algorithms on standardized test problems                                     algorithm has better performance. In summary, MaOEA-TWD is
WFGi (i = 1,2. . . 6). Among the 30 comparison results obtained,                                  superior to other algorithms.
MaOEA-TWD has obtained the optimal solution for 14 times, per-                                        In Table 6, GD is the convergence index, the smaller the GD
forming well on data sets WFG1, WFG2, and WFG4. Moreover,                                         value, the better the solution set convergence, Spacing is the diver-
when the target number M is equal to 8, the algorithm performs                                    sity index, the smaller the Spacing value, the better the solution
better, while GrEA has obtained the optimal solution for 12 times,                                diversity. When the objective is 6, the convergence and diversity
with GrEA obtaining the most optimal solutions. The reason is that                                of the MaOEA-TWD algorithm on the test problem DTLZ5 are the
the GrEA algorithm uses a grid to enhance the selection pressure.                                 best, the convergence and diversity of the GrEA algorithm are bet-
Two times the RVEA algorithm is equal to the optimal solution,                                    ter, when the objective is 8, the convergence and diversity of the
and seven times the solution is better than the MaOEA-TWD. This                                   MaOEA-TWD algorithm are the best, the convergence and diversity
is due to RVEA’s scaling method of angle penalty distance to bal-                                 of the GrEA algorithm become worse, the convergence and diver-
ance the convergence and diversity of the solutions in the high-                                  sity of the EFRRR algorithm become better, when the objective
dimensional target space. NSGA-III obtained the optimal solution                                  reaches 15, the convergence and diversity of the MaOEA-TWD
twice, and MaOEA-TWD was superior to Maoea-TwD 9 times,                                           algorithm are the best, the diversity is better, The GrEA algorithm
mainly because NSGA-III used the reference point strategy to bal-                                 has good convergence but poor diversity, and the EFRRR algorithm
ance the diversity and convergence of the solutions. WFG1 has                                     has the best diversity but poor convergence. The algorithms RVEA,
the characteristic of mixed bias, WFG2 is a discrete curve, and                                   MaOEARD and NSGA-III have poor convergence and diversity
WFG4 is a concave problem. MaOEA-TWD has dealt with these dif-                                    under different objectives. Therefore, the MaOEA-TWD algorithm
ficulties effectively, striking a good balance between the diversity                              can better balance convergence and diversity, and has better per-
and convergence of algorithms. The main reason is that the selec-                                 formance advantages.

    In Table 7, GD is a convergence indicator. The smaller the GD                             Declaration of Competing Interest
value, the better the convergence of the solution set. Spacing is a
diversity indicator. The smaller the Spacing value, the better the                                The authors declare that they have no known competing finan-
solution set diversity. When the target was 6, MaOEA-TWD algo-                                cial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared
rithm had the best convergence and diversity on the test prob-                                to influence the work reported in this paper.
lem WFG2, while GrEA and RVEA algorithms had better
convergence and diversity. When the target was 8, MaOEA-
TWD algorithm had the best convergence and diversity, while                                   Acknowledgments

algorithm has good convergence and diversity. When the target                                    This work was supported by the National Natural Science Foun-

and RVEA algorithm has the worst convergence and diversity.                                   Research Innovation Fund - Future Network Innovation Research

advantages.
